main_findings: Direct architectural symbol spotting work uses tile-based detector training/inference to address tiny symbols in large drawings.
CAD/FloorPlanCAD literature uses graph/CNN-GCN context for panoptic symbol spotting, suggesting our score-only selector is under-featured.
Small-object detection literature supports SAHI-style slicing, but duplicate suppression and inflation control are critical.
Multi-task floorplan recognition uses boundary/room context, supporting a context-aware symbol selector rather than isolated bbox policies.
Reported AP/PQ/mIoU numbers are not directly comparable to our P101 target-level F1 and should only be used as related work, not a SOTA claim.
recommended_next_tasks: P171 sliced inference plus merge policy
P172 context-aware selector with room/wall/text-neighborhood features
P173 class-balanced specialist training for shower/column/stair
